title: Zero should not be a possible denominator
description: Zero should not be a possible denominator. Analyze your code intentionality-logical reliability. Bug cwe symbolic-execution denial-of-service cert. Ensure that integer division and remainder operations do not result in divide-by-zero errors. Why is this an issue? What is the potential impact? How can I fix it? More Info: If the denominator to a division or modulo operation is zero, the behavior of the application is undefined. Operator / is used for division and % for modulo operation. Division and modulo operations are susceptible to divide-by-zero (and signed integer overflow) errors. Available In: Catch issues on the fly, in your IDE. Detect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: int divide(int a, int b) {   return a / b; // Noncompliant: no check for b == 0 }  int modulo(int x, int y) {   if (y == 0) {     x = 1;   }   return x % y; // Noncompliant: y could still be 0 }  int calculate(int numerator) {   int denominator = getValue(); // unknown value   return numerator / denominator; // Noncompliant: could be 0 }
compliant: int divide(int a, int b) {   if (b != 0) {     return a / b; // Compliant: check prevents division by zero   }   return 0; // or handle error appropriately }  int modulo(int x, int y) {   if (y != 0) {     return x % y; // Compliant: check prevents modulo by zero   }   return 0; }  int calculate(int numerator) {   int denominator = getValue();   if (denominator != 0) {     return numerator / denominator; // Compliant: validated   }   return INT_MAX; // error value }

checks.message: Potential division by zero: denominator "$2" could be zero.
Potential modulo by zero: denominator "$2" could be zero.
